sections.heading: Status
sections.content:
Phase: builder
Baseline SHA: ca6010e868590f86b2d34523137b3a72fd219d23
Started: 2026-03-23
Cycle ID: pev-2026-03-23-semantic-search

Phase transitions:
- planning: 2026-03-23 — cycle created
- builder: 2026-03-23 — plan approved

sections.heading: Request
sections.content: add semantic search to cortex — I want to be able to search doc sections and code docstrings by meaning, not just keywords. Something with embeddings, maybe RAG-style retrieval. Not sure what the right approach is for a local-first tool with an SQLite backend.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content:
Modules affected: `cortex/index/db.py` (schema, embedding storage, search), `cortex/mcp_server.py` (cortex_search tool), `cortex/index/builder.py` (embedding generation during build).

New module: an embeddings helper (likely `cortex/index/embeddings.py`) to encapsulate model loading and vector generation.

Scope decision: Single-feature. This adds a new search capability layered on top of the existing FTS5 infrastructure. The embedding index lives alongside the existing node_fts table in cortex.db. No architectural decisions needed beyond what is captured in this pitch.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.content: Cortex search (`cortex_search`) currently uses SQLite FTS5 full-text matching, which requires exact or prefix word matches. This fails for conceptual queries — searching "how does staleness work" won't find nodes described as "detects content drift via hash comparison." Additionally, doc sections (635 rows in `doc_sections`) are not searchable at all through `cortex_search`; only the ~1500 code nodes in `node_fts` are indexed. Users must know the exact doc ID to read documentation, making discovery difficult for both humans and agents.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
1. **As an agent using cortex_search, I want to find relevant nodes using natural-language queries** so that I can locate code and documentation without knowing exact identifier names. Acceptance: a query like "hash comparison for detecting changes" returns staleness-related nodes even though those exact words don't appear in node summaries. A new `mode` parameter (or similar) on `cortex_search` selects semantic search.

2. **As an agent, I want doc sections to appear in cortex_search results** so that I can discover documentation without knowing doc IDs. Acceptance: `cortex_search(query="...", node_type="doc_section")` or a filter parameter returns matching doc sections with their full section IDs. Works for both FTS and semantic modes.

3. **As a developer, I want embeddings generated automatically during `cortex build`** so that the semantic index stays current without manual steps. Acceptance: after `cortex build`, new and changed nodes have embeddings. Unchanged nodes keep their existing embeddings (no redundant recomputation).

4. **As a developer, I want the embedding model to run locally with no API keys or network access** so that semantic search works offline and has no per-query cost. Acceptance: a small local model (e.g., sentence-transformers `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` or similar) generates embeddings. The model is downloaded once on first use. Total disk footprint under 100MB.

sections.heading: Solution Sketch
sections.content:
The approach is local embeddings with sqlite-vec, unified into cortex_search with mode and scope parameters.

**New module: `cortex/index/embeddings.py`** — handles model loading (sentence-transformers, small model like all-MiniLM-L6-v2) and text-to-vector conversion. Model lazily loaded on first semantic query to avoid blocking MCP startup.

**DB layer (`cortex/index/db.py`)** — adds a sqlite-vec virtual table for vector storage in cortex.db. sqlite-vec extension loaded on every connection open. Embeddings keyed by node ID with a content hash to skip re-embedding unchanged entries.

**Builder (`cortex/index/builder.py`)** — embeds level_1+level_2 for code nodes and heading+content for doc sections during build. Batch embedding in chunks for memory efficiency. Doc sections also added to FTS5 index (closing existing gap).

**MCP tool (`cortex/mcp_server.py`)** — `cortex_search` gains a `mode` parameter (keyword|semantic|hybrid, default: keyword) and a `scope` parameter (code|docs|all, default: all). Semantic mode embeds the query and finds nearest neighbors via sqlite-vec. Hybrid mode combines FTS ranking with vector similarity.

**Dependencies (`pyproject.toml`)** — adds sentence-transformers (or fastembed) and sqlite-vec as new dependencies.

Edge cases: graceful degradation if sqlite-vec unavailable; FTS fallback stages unchanged in keyword mode; doc section IDs use qualified format consistently.

sections.heading: Constraints
sections.content:
**Rabbit holes — do not go here:**

- Do not implement hybrid search (combining FTS and vector scores) in this cycle. Offer FTS and semantic as separate modes. Hybrid ranking is a future enhancement.
- Do not build a custom embedding model or fine-tune anything. Use an off-the-shelf sentence-transformers model.
- Do not add semantic search to the viz dashboard in this cycle. MCP tool only.
- Do not implement incremental re-embedding triggered by file watches or git hooks. Embeddings update during `cortex build` only.

**No-gos — explicitly out of scope:**

- No cloud/API-based embedding services. Local model only, no network calls after initial model download.
- No changes to the existing FTS5 search behavior. FTS must continue to work exactly as it does today when semantic mode is not requested.
- No new MCP tools. Doc section search and semantic search are integrated into the existing `cortex_search` tool via parameters.

**Trade-offs accepted:**

- First semantic query in a session will be slow (model loading). This is acceptable for a local tool.
- The embedding model adds ~80-100MB to disk. Acceptable for the capability it provides.
- sqlite-vec is a relatively new extension. If platform compatibility issues arise, the Builder should make the feature optional (graceful degradation to FTS-only).

**Test budget:** 5-8 focused tests covering: vector table creation/migration, embedding generation for nodes and doc sections, semantic search returning ranked results, doc section FTS indexing, graceful degradation when sqlite-vec is unavailable, cortex_search parameter handling for mode and type filtering.
